content: Thank you, that distinction is genuinely helpful and not one I'd thought through carefully enough on my first pass. On reflection, for second-line review purposes I would want that manual-review bucket added, because in the case where the tool can technically assign a label but the underlying contact is ambiguous — say a caller raising both a fee question and hinting at a possible unauthorised charge — a confidently-assigned single category could understate the matter and we'd lose the opportunity for a human reviewer to look again before the population is closed out. The precondition I'd attach is that the manual-review file should sit alongside the fraud-flagged file in terms of handling: not forwarded to the regional leads in the standard distribution, retained with original interaction identifiers, and counted toward our reconciliation total so the in-period contact count still ties out. Should we encounter a borderline matter that touches both unauthorised-access indicators and an ordinary category, my preference would be that the fraud flag takes precedence and it routes to the fraud team rather than the manual-review set, since their evidentiary handling is the stricter of the two. Could you add that bucket on those terms?
